name: Reactive & Semantic Token Graph

userStory.iWant: to define my theme using a graph of related colors
userStory.soThat: changing one brand color automatically cascades through my entire UI and adapts to light/dark terminal backgrounds.

acceptanceCriteria: Tokens can be defined as References to other tokens (Aliases)
Tokens can be derived using functional math (darken, lighten, mix, invert)
Tokens can have conditional buckets for 'light' and 'dark' terminal backgrounds
A reactive stream/observable exists to signal when any part of the graph changes
backwardCompatibility: Current hex-based TokenValue definitions will be the 'leaf nodes' of the graph, ensuring all existing themes continue to work unchanged.

inScope: Support for cycle detection in references
outScope: Individual component logic (they just resolve the token from the graph)

testPlan.goldenPath: Define a 'primary' color. Define 'border' as a 20% darkened 'primary'. Change 'primary' and assert that 'border' resolves to the new correct darkened color.
edgeCases: Deep reference chains (e.g. A -> B -> C -> D)
testPlan.fuzzStress: Build a graph with 10,000 nodes and 50,000 edges and measure the time to re-resolve the entire graph after a root change.
failureCases: Infinite recursion during reference resolution (circular dependencies)

estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(V + E) where V is tokens and E is references
